title: Localisation hooks (content extraction, no translation yet)
context: Full translation of patient-facing health content is a future track (task 503) that is itself high-risk and requires re-review per language. Before that work is possible, the site architecture must support localisation: content strings extractable, locale routing in place, and the build pipeline ready to accept translated content. This task adds the infrastructure hooks only — no translations are produced here.
objective: Add localisation infrastructure hooks to the static site — content extraction, locale routing, and build pipeline support — without producing any translations, so that future translation work (task 503) can be dropped in without architecture changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Content strings are extracted from Markdown modules, worksheets, and UI elements into a localisation-ready format (e.g. i18n JSON/YAML resource files or front-matter locale keys).
Locale routing is configured in the static-site generator: the English (en) locale is the default and the only one initially populated.

Build pipeline accepts additional locale directories and processes them alongside English without failing; a placeholder 'es' or 'fr' stub can be added and processed by the build (even with English fallback) to prove the hooks work.
A CONTRIBUTING note documents how to add a new locale and what the translation review requirements are (pointing to the high-risk policy in task 002).
No translations are produced or committed; the English content is unchanged.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Scope — localisation hooks; § Out of scope — full translation in v1)
output: A merged PR adding localisation infrastructure hooks (content extraction, locale routing, build pipeline) to the static site, with a documented process for adding locales, and no translations produced.
